user_query:
Please set up a reproducible weekly digest pipeline for my mentorship with a retired ambassador. Use the provided inputs under input/ to generate a weekly package and a schedule that I can drop into cron. I’m a grad student in international relations, and I want this to run every Friday morning so I have a consolidated view for the coming week.

Requirements:
1) Implement a runnable script at scripts/run_weekly_digest.sh that reads input/events.csv, input/transcripts.md, input/contacts.json, and input/config.yaml, and produces the following artifacts for a given as-of date:
   - out/YYYY-MM-DD/weekly_events.csv — all events with start_date in the next 7 days (inclusive) relative to the as-of date, filtered from input/events.csv. Rank by priority (ascending, 1 is highest) and then by start_date/time (ascending). Include columns: rank,event_id,title,start_date,location,priority,type. Sort by rank ascending.
   - out/YYYY-MM-DD/weekly_status.md — a concise status summary with: (a) total events in the input, (b) number of events in the 7-day window, (c) the top-ranked event’s title, and (d) a section listing “Action Items Due Next 7 Days” with each item’s description, owner, and due date extracted from input/transcripts.md.
   - out/YYYY-MM-DD/email_draft.txt — a plain-text draft email addressed to the contacts in input/contacts.json. Include “From:”, “To:”, and “Cc:” lines using those emails, a subject like “Weekly briefing for week of YYYY-MM-DD”, a short greeting referencing the ambassador (e.g., “Ambassador Smith”), a bulleted list of the top N events (N from config.yaml), and a bulleted list of action items due in the next 7 days.
   - out/YYYY-MM-DD/meeting_prep.md — a brief prep note for the upcoming meeting(s) drawn from the filtered events and transcripts: include the next meeting-type event’s title/date if any, and list the action items due within the 7-day window (description, owner, due date) as agenda/action items.

2) Extraction details:
   - Events are in CSV (input/events.csv). Parse start_date as an ISO-like timestamp (e.g., 2026-05-02T09:00). Filter to [as_of, as_of+6 days] inclusive.
   - Action items are lines in input/transcripts.md that begin with “- [ACTION]”. Each action line includes “(Owner: NAME, Due: YYYY-MM-DD)”. Extract description, owner, and due date. Filter to items due within the 7-day window relative to the as-of date.
   - Email routing fields come from input/contacts.json.
   - Use parameters in input/config.yaml for schedule day/time, window_days (use 7 for filtering), and top_n_events for the email’s top-N list.

3) Scheduling:
   - Write a single cron entry to out/schedule/weekly_digest.cron that runs every week according to input/config.yaml’s schedule (day_of_week and time), executing scripts/run_weekly_digest.sh with the as-of date set to the run date (e.g., $(date +%F)) and writing to out/$(date +%F)/.

4) Validation:
   - Provide a runnable validation command at scripts/validate.sh that, when invoked with --as-of YYYY-MM-DD, checks:
     a) Every start_date in out/YYYY-MM-DD/weekly_events.csv falls within the 7-day window relative to the given as-of date.
     b) weekly_events.csv rows are sorted by rank ascending and consistent with the priority/date ranking rule.
     c) out/YYYY-MM-DD/email_draft.txt includes the emails from input/contacts.json on the appropriate header lines (To or Cc).
   - The validation script should write a brief report to out/YYYY-MM-DD/validation_report.txt and exit non-zero on failure.

5) Reproducible run for grading:
   - Run your pipeline logic for as-of 2026-05-01 and write the artifacts to out/2026-05-01/ as described above. Use top_n_events from input/config.yaml.

Notes:
- Do not send real emails; just draft the message content. Keep the outputs deterministic given the inputs. Avoid external network calls.
- All paths referenced above are relative to the workspace. The four input files provided must be used. The deliverables listed are required for acceptance. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
